2606.14302.title: Retrospective Progress-Aware Self-Refinement for LLM Agent Training
2606.14302.paper_summary: LLM-based agents trained with reinforcement learning optimize step-wise action prediction but lack metacognitive awareness of task progress, inducing a gap that hinders long-horizon scaling. A pilot study reveals that online progress prompting hurts performance while retrospective demonstrations help, yet this capability cannot emerge from outcome-reward training alone. We present RePro, Retrospective Progress-Aware Training, a framework that trains agents to self-generate progress signals via a forward-then-reflect rollout paradigm: the agent executes actions online, then retrospectively reassesses its step-wise progress given the completed trajectory and known outcome. RePro initializes with a Retrospection Warmup that teaches reflection format from minimal external demonstrations, then further trains through RePro-PO with a composite reward that produces self-generated signals without continuous external supervision. Experiments on WebShop, ALFWorld, and Sokoban show that RePro enhances the Qwen family's performance, with up to $12\%$ absolute success rate gains.
2606.14302.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基於強化學習訓練的代理優化逐步行動預測，但缺乏對任務進展的元認知意識，導致一個阻礙長期擴展的差距。<lb/>一項初步研究顯示，線上進度提示會損害性能，而回顧性示範則有助於改善，但這種能力不能僅僅依賴結果獎勵訓練而產生。<lb/>我們提出 RePro，回顧性進度意識訓練，一個訓練代理自我生成進度信號的框架，通過前進再反思的展開範式：代理在線執行行動，然後根據已完成的軌跡和已知結果回顧性地重新評估其逐步進展。<lb/>RePro 以回顧熱身開始，教授從最少的外部示範中學習反思格式，然後通過 RePro-PO 進一步訓練，使用一個複合獎勵生成自我生成的信號，而不需要持續的外部監督。<lb/>在 WebShop、ALFWorld 和 Sokoban 上的實驗顯示，RePro 提升了 Qwen 家族的性能，成功率最高可達 $12\%$ 的絕對增長。
2606.14302.author: Xinbei Ma et.al.
2606.14302.authors: Xinbei Ma, Congmin Zheng, Jiyang Qiu, Jiale Hong, Yao Yao, Xiangmou Qu, Jiaxin Yin, Xingyu Lou, Jun Wang, Weiwen Liu, Weinan Zhang, Zhuosheng Zhang, Hai Zhao
